hero.headline: Support work with proof in automation, feedback intelligence, and friction analysis.
hero.subheadline: External-first public pack for Support Ops and Technical Support roles, using selected Heapp Games case studies as proof of leverage rather than a public product pivot.

projects.title: Darkest AFK - Support Tooling for Faster Player Recovery
projects.context: Support and admin workflows needed a faster, safer way to prepare compensation packages without repeated manual lookup and formatting.
projects.problem: Manual item search and package prep created repetitive friction and made consistency harder to maintain.
method: Built a static operator tool with search, filtering, bilingual labels, and standardized package-prep logic
Optimized the flow for repetitive internal support work rather than presentation-only browsing
Created a cleaner path from item lookup to repeatable output
insights: Operator tooling matters when the same recovery flow happens repeatedly
Consistency is as important as speed in compensation work
Workflow design can remove support friction without changing team structure
projects.decision: Package the story publicly as support automation, not as a product-build narrative.
impact: 112+ indexed items in one operator-facing tool
Clearer and more repeatable package preparation
Clear proof of support tooling and process thinking
nextExperiment: Measure package-prep time before and after tool adoption
Track formatting mistakes and repeat corrections
Identify the next repetitive support workflow worth standardizing

projects.title: Dig Dig Die - Multi-Source Feedback Intelligence and Prioritization
projects.context: Steam, Discord, and community reports contained recurring bug and UX signals, but the feedback was fragmented and duplicated across channels.
projects.problem: Support and product conversations were slower because the same issues were appearing in multiple formats without a shared structure.
method: Clustered raw feedback into repeat issue groups and feedback themes
Added simple priority framing to move from noise to next actions
Packed the result into a support-to-product escalation view
insights: 23 structured items made the volume of player pain legible
6 critical bug or UX issues stood out as near-term risk
3 repeat feedback themes showed where user frustration was concentrating
projects.decision: Publish the work as customer insight translation and prioritization quality, not as a raw data artifact.
impact: Faster path from public user complaints to structured escalation
Less duplication between support, product, and dev conversations
Clear proof of feedback intelligence inside a support-led narrative
nextExperiment: Track whether structured escalations shorten prioritization conversations
Measure how often duplicate complaints collapse into a single issue view
Repeat the same workflow for another noisy signal set

projects.title: Vacation Cafe Simulator - Retention and Friction Analysis
projects.context: Community and market feedback described points where the cozy loop started to feel repetitive or mechanically heavy, but the signals were not yet organized into actions.
projects.problem: The underlying player pain was real, but it was scattered across channels and not translated into testable next steps.
method: Grouped repeat complaints into friction patterns instead of isolated anecdotes
Mapped the patterns to simple retention and session-quality questions
Proposed short-loop hypotheses a team could review quickly
insights: A repeat friction signal appeared around 6-8 hours for part of the player base
Complexity increased near Level 4-5 and changed the feel of the loop
This work is strongest as decision support, not as a full product-title pivot
projects.decision: Keep the work in the pack as evidence of analytical leverage within a support-ops identity.
impact: Clear public example of friction analysis and hypothesis framing
Better language for turning user pain into support-to-product escalation
Useful differentiator without breaking the support narrative
nextExperiment: Test whether the same framework helps in a SaaS onboarding or help-center context
Shorten the analysis into a one-page operating memo
Track whether teams act faster when friction patterns are named clearly

roadmap.focus: Best fit for teams that want fewer repetitive steps, cleaner processes, and stronger day-to-day support execution.
roadmap.kpi: Proof: Darkest AFK tooling, Mover automation, and knowledge operations work

roadmap.focus: Best fit for products with complex journeys, debugging-heavy support, and a real need for structured issue translation.
roadmap.kpi: Proof: bridge incident handling, QA-style escalation, and Dig Dig Die signal clustering

roadmap.focus: Best fit for roles where support needs to protect trust, guide users clearly, and convert recurring issues into better decisions.
roadmap.kpi: Proof: onboarding guidance, incident communication, and retention-friction insight work

narrative: This pack is built for external interviews first. The headline, summary logic, and project framing stay aligned across the resume, LinkedIn, Hirify, and portfolio.
The Heapp Games examples highlight operator thinking, customer insight translation, and analytical leverage while omitting private workflows and internal-only data.
Open to remote-first Support Ops, Technical Support, Customer Success, and support-heavy roles in Web3, FinTech, SaaS, and gamedev.
